entries.title: Crusher is back as Enterprise-D chief medical officer in season 3

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 3, Memory Alpha story rows 1-26, runtime episode aliases 1-26, primary on-screen Prime continuity.
continuity.notes: Draft reference deck. Excludes later TNG seasons, TNG films, DS9, Voyager, Picard-era material, beta canon, and fanon unless a card is an explicit future guard.

content.fact: Season 3 restores Beverly Crusher as chief medical officer, replacing the Pulaski season and reestablishing Crusher family and medical dynamics aboard the Enterprise-D.
content.injection: Write Beverly Crusher as active Enterprise-D CMO in TNG season 3. Do not keep Pulaski aboard as the regular ship doctor unless a scene explicitly references season 2.

content.fact: By season 3, Picard, Riker, Data, Worf, Geordi, Troi, Crusher, Wesley, and Guinan function as a more settled Enterprise-D ensemble.
content.injection: Use TNG season 3 as a mature Enterprise-D baseline: roles are clearer, relationships are more practiced, and episode gates override only where they narrow this state.

entries.title: Data must persuade colonists who do not respond to authority alone

context.label: TNG season 3: The Ensigns of Command

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 3, Memory Alpha story rows 1-26, runtime episode aliases 1-26, primary on-screen Prime continuity.
continuity.notes: Draft reference deck. Excludes later TNG seasons, TNG films, DS9, Voyager, Picard-era material, beta canon, and fanon unless a card is an explicit future guard.

content.fact: On Tau Cygna V, Data cannot rely on rank or android strength; he has to learn how to persuade stubborn colonists before the Sheliak deadline.
content.injection: Use The Ensigns of Command to show Data learning social persuasion as a practical command skill, not just technical competence.
